sections.content:
RIGHT didn't change the board (board_changed: false). The player is still at cols 24-26. The top frame is at cols 28-31. RIGHT would move player to cols 28-30, which overlaps the frame. But it didn't work - maybe the blue target inside the frame (at rows 29-30, cols 29-30) is blocking?
Let me think about this differently. The player is at rows 28-31, cols 24-26. The top frame is at rows 28-31, cols 28-31. The blue target is at rows 29-30, cols 29-30. If the player moves RIGHT to cols 28-30, the player would be at rows 28-31, cols 28-30. The blue target at rows 29-30, cols 29-30 would be inside the player's area. Maybe the blue target blocks movement?
Wait, but earlier when I moved into the left frame (which also had a blue target inside), it worked. Let me check - the left frame was at rows 32-35, cols 24-27 with blue target at rows 33-34, cols 25-26. The player moved from cols 21-23 to cols 25-27, which overlapped the frame and broke it. So blue targets don't block.
Hmm, but the player is at rows 28-31, cols 24-26. RIGHT moves +4 to cols 28-30. But the top frame is at cols 28-31. The player would be at cols 28-30, which overlaps cols 28-30 of the frame. But the frame is hollow (4x4 hollow square), so the interior is empty except for the blue target. The player at cols 28-30 would overlap with the frame's left wall at col 28 and the interior at cols 29-30.
Wait, maybe the issue is that the player is already at the same rows as the frame (rows 28-31), and RIGHT tries to move into the frame's space but can't because the frame's wall is in the way? But earlier, moving into the left frame worked...
Let me reconsider. The left frame was at rows 32-35, cols 24-27. The player was at rows 32-35, cols 21-23 (same rows). RIGHT moved to cols 25-27, which overlapped the frame. But wait - the frame is at cols 24-27, so the left wall is at col 24. The player at cols 25-27 would be inside the frame (cols 25-26 are interior, col 27 is right wall). So the player moved past the left wall into the frame.
For the top frame at cols 28-31: the left wall is at col 28. The player at cols 24-26, RIGHT to cols 28-30. The player's left edge would be at col 28 (the frame's left wall). So the player would be at cols 28-30, with the frame's left wall at col 28. This should work the same way...
Unless the white strip at col 27 is blocking? The white strip is at rows 28-31, col 27. The player is at cols 24-26, so the white strip is adjacent but not overlapping. RIGHT moves to cols 28-30, which skips col 27. So the white strip shouldn't block.
Maybe the blue bar at cols 32-39 is somehow affecting this? Or maybe the frame at cols 28-31 has a different interaction because the blue target is at cols 29-30, which is where the player would be (cols 28-30 includes cols 29-30).
Actually wait - let me re-examine. The player is a 4x3 rectangle (rows 28-31, cols 24-26). RIGHT moves +4 cols to cols 28-30. The top frame is a 4x4 hollow square at rows 28-31, cols 28-31. The frame's left wall is at col 28, right wall at col 31, top wall at row 28, bottom wall at row 31. The interior is rows 29-30, cols 29-30, which contains the blue target.
When the player moves to cols 28-30, the player occupies rows 28-31, cols 28-30. This overlaps with:
- Frame left wall at col 28 (rows 28-31)
- Frame top wall at row 28 (cols 28-31)
- Frame bottom wall at row 31 (cols 28-31)
- Frame interior at rows 29-30, cols 29-30
So the player would overlap the frame walls. This should break the frame, same as before. But it didn't work (board_changed: false).
Hmm, maybe there's something different about this frame. Let me check if the frame is actually at a different position or if the player position is different than I think.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the segmentation output more carefully:
- N: [[28, 24], [28, 26], [31, 26], [31, 24]] - this means the player's boundary corners are at (28,24), (28,26), (31,26), (31,24). So rows 28-31, cols 24-26.
- The top frame c: [[28, 28], [28, 31], [31, 31], [31, 28]] - rows 28-31, cols 28-31.
Player at cols 24-26, RIGHT to cols 28-30. The frame is at cols 28-31. The player would be at cols 28-30, overlapping cols 28-30 of the frame.
But the move didn't work. Maybe the blue bar at cols 32-39 is connected to the frame somehow? Or maybe the player can't move RIGHT because the frame is in the way?
Wait, I just realized something. The player is at rows 28-31, cols 24-26. The frame is at rows 28-31, cols 28-31. Between them is col 27, which has the white strip. The player is 3 cols wide (cols 24-26). RIGHT moves +4 cols, so the new position would be cols 28-30. But col 27 is between the current and new positions. 
In the earlier case with the left frame: player at cols 21-23, frame at cols 24-27. RIGHT moves to cols 25-27. The player's new position (cols 25-27) overlaps the frame (cols 24-27). The player moved past col 24 (frame's left wall) into cols 25-27.
For the top frame: player at cols 24-26, frame at cols 28-31. RIGHT to cols 28-30. The player's new position starts at col 28, which is the frame's left wall. The player would be at cols 28-30, which is inside the frame (cols 28-30 of a 28-31 frame).
But the move didn't work. Maybe the game considers this as "moving into" the frame, which is blocked? But the earlier case also moved into the frame...
Actually, I wonder if the issue is that the player is at the SAME rows as the frame. In the left frame case, the player was also at the same rows (rows 32-35). So that shouldn't matter.
Let me try a different approach. What if I try to go around? Like UP first (but the player is already at rows 28-31, so UP would go to rows 25-28). Or DOWN to rows 32-35, then RIGHT, then UP.
Actually, let me try DOWN to get to rows 32-35, then RIGHT to break the left frame again (it's at rows 32-35, cols 24-27). The player is at cols 24-26, which already overlaps the left frame at cols 24-27! Wait, the player is at rows 28-31, cols 24-26. The left frame is at rows 32-35, cols 24-27. Different rows, so no overlap.
If I go DOWN, player moves to rows 32-35, cols 24-26. The left frame is at rows 32-35, cols 24-27. The player at cols 24-26 overlaps the frame at cols 24-26! This should break the left frame!
Let me try DOWN.
